document: Cloud Sovereignty Framework - Implementation guidance (EU Commission, post-tender, undated)

label.en: ECSF Implementation Guidance alternative weight matrix

note.en: Differs from v1.2.1's default weights (SOV-1 15, SOV-5 20, SOV-7 10; see ecsf-scoring.json) only in SOV-1 (+5), SOV-5 (-10) and SOV-7 (+5); sum is 100 in both sets. The guidance text states authorities may adapt these values. This is also the exact weight set used by the official calculator XLSX's own D-column (cross-checked in Phase 2b.1) — the calculator does not use the v1.2.1 default weights.

source_pointer.document: Cloud Sovereignty Framework - Implementation guidance (EU Commission, post-tender, undated)

description.en: The government's overall SEAL level for an assessment is the minimum (lowest) SEAL level achieved across all assessed objectives, not an average or weighted score.

source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative.

generalized_seal_2.en: An autonomous entity in its organization, but not in its technical choices. The service continues but no longer has access to updates and security patches in the event of a break in access to the underlying technology.

generalized_seal_3.en: Access to the roadmap. Complete guarantee of operations continuity.

generalized_seal_4.en: Decision-making centres exclusively in the {TRUSTED_REGION}. Priority {TRUSTED_REGION} customers in roadmap arbitrations.

source_pointer.document: Cloud Sovereignty Framework - Implementation guidance (EU Commission, post-tender, undated)
source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative.

generalized_seal_2.en: Isolation by creating separate entities. Limited exposure to export control-type measures.

generalized_seal_3.en: Complete insulation guaranteeing the inapplicability of legislation from outside {TRUSTED_REGION}. No exposure of {NATION} to export control measures.

generalized_seal_4.en: Operations are designed and carried out exclusively in the {TRUSTED_REGION}. Protection of international institutions against export control measures.

source_pointer.document: Cloud Sovereignty Framework - Implementation guidance (EU Commission, post-tender, undated)
source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative.

generalized_seal_2.en: Full control of the data, including encryption control, data localization and deletion guarantee. Logs available.

generalized_seal_3.en: {TRUSTED_REGION} design AI. Logs recorded in real time in the {TRUSTED_REGION}.

generalized_seal_4.en: Immutability of logs, audits carried out by {TRUSTED_REGION} teams.

source_pointer.document: Cloud Sovereignty Framework - Implementation guidance (EU Commission, post-tender, undated)
source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative.

generalized_seal_2.en: Operations carried out and documented locally. Expertise from outside the {TRUSTED_REGION} may be necessary. Open and documented alternatives exist.

generalized_seal_3.en: Availability of expertise in Europe, including subcontractors. Processes are designed and documented locally.

generalized_seal_4.en: Complete {TRUSTED_REGION} autonomy, including security clearances and the integration of key skills of subcontractors.

source_pointer.document: Cloud Sovereignty Framework - Implementation guidance (EU Commission, post-tender, undated)
source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative.

generalized_seal_2.en: Majority of the supply chains are documented. Deployments are carried out locally, according to procedures that can be external. Critical suppliers and subcontractors can be audited.

generalized_seal_3.en: The majority of services are designed in the {TRUSTED_REGION}. They are deployed and orchestrated locally. No subcontractors involved in critical services.

generalized_seal_4.en: {TRUSTED_REGION}-certified components origin. {TRUSTED_REGION} design, build and compliance check. No dependence on suppliers from outside {TRUSTED_REGION}. Complete auditability of suppliers and subcontractors.

source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative.

generalized_seal_2.en: The services are partially interoperable. HPC is hosted on-premises.

generalized_seal_3.en: {TRUSTED_REGION} and public standards for core services. Open-source majority and predominance of {TRUSTED_REGION} contributors.

generalized_seal_4.en: Auditability of the architecture. Full compliance: {TRUSTED_REGION} Open AI, public standard, open source.

source_pointer.section: The Sovereign Cloud Framework Matrix — SEAL-2/3/4 requirements table

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative. Additionally: the SEAL-3 cell reads "ELA 3." in the source PDF, which is almost certainly a typo for "EAL 3" (Evaluation Assurance Level, matching the "EAL2"/"EAL 4-5" phrasing used in the neighboring SEAL-2/SEAL-4 cells of this same row). Preserved verbatim as printed per project convention (working rule 2 — never silently correct source text); erratum candidate, alongside BSI SOV-4-02-C2 (Phase 2a) and the Article 18/19 cross-reference (Phase 2c).

generalized_seal_2.en: EAL2 level security. Local operations, transparent and immediate feedback of information, audits allowed.

generalization_note.en: Erratum override: verbatim source reads 'ELA 3.' — a probable source typo for 'EAL 3' (Evaluation Assurance Level), matching the 'EAL2'/'EAL 4-5' phrasing in the same row's SEAL-2/SEAL-4 cells (see this entry's own needs_review_note). generalized_seal_3 corrects this to 'EAL 3.'; the verbatim seal_3 field (isolated in data/local/) is left unmodified per working rule 2.

domain_seal_requirements.needs_review_note: SEAL-2/3/4 column boundaries in this row are inferred from sentence/punctuation breaks in the guidance PDF's reflowed text (source page 10); the original table's cell geometry was not preserved by text extraction. Verify against the source PDF table layout before treating this split as authoritative. SOV-8's SEAL-3 cell has no text distinguishable from SEAL-2/SEAL-4 in the source reflow and is omitted rather than guessed. Additionally: the source row for SOV-8 prints only two visually distinct cells across the SEAL-2/3/4 columns, not three — this extraction assigns the first cell to SEAL-2 and the second to SEAL-4 based on sentence content, but this seal_2/seal_4 assignment is an interpretation, not a direct reading of confirmed column boundaries; the source PDF's table geometry has not been visually confirmed against the extracted text for this row specifically.
